entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: An opportunistic fungal pathogen of plants like maize and peanut that produces aflatoxins.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop that serves as a host for Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop that serves as a host for Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: An environmental condition that promotes the growth of Aspergillus flavus and the production of aflatoxins.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The biological capability of Aspergillus flavus to produce toxic aflatoxin compounds.

entities.name: OXIDATIVE STRESS
entities.description: A condition characterized by an imbalance of reactive oxygen species, which exacerbates aflatoxin production.

entities.name: PROTEOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of proteins used to analyze the responses of Aspergillus flavus to oxidative stress.

entities.name: H2O2-DERIVED OXIDATIVE STRESS
entities.description: Oxidative stress specifically induced by hydrogen peroxide used in experimental cultures.

entities.name: BIOMARKERS
entities.description: Biological indicators identified through proteomic analysis to aid in host resistance breeding.

entities.name: HOST RESISTANCE BREEDING
entities.description: The agricultural practice of developing plant varieties resistant to pathogen infection.

entities.name: AF13
entities.description: An isolate of Aspergillus flavus characterized by high aflatoxin production and high oxidative stress tolerance.

entities.name: NRRL3357
entities.description: An isolate of Aspergillus flavus characterized by moderate aflatoxin production.

entities.name: K54A
entities.description: An isolate of Aspergillus flavus characterized by no aflatoxin production.

entities.name: ITRAQ
entities.description: Isobaric Tags for Relative and Absolute Quantification, a proteomic method used to identify differentially expressed proteins.

entities.name: DIFFERENTIALLY EXPRESSED PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins that show significant changes in expression levels in response to oxidative stress.

entities.name: METABOLIC PATHWAYS
entities.description: Biochemical pathways including antioxidants, carbohydrates, and secondary metabolism involved in the pathogen's response.

entities.name: PATHOGENICITY
entities.description: The ability of Aspergillus flavus to cause disease in its plant hosts.

entities.name: SECONDARY METABOLISM
entities.description: The production of secondary metabolites, which is increased in high-aflatoxin producing isolates under stress.

entities.name: LYTIC ENZYME
entities.description: Enzymes that break down substrates, whose increased expression in AF13 correlates with oxidative stress tolerance.

entities.name: SECONDARY METABOLITE
entities.description: Compounds produced via secondary metabolism that assist in plant infection and microbial competition.

entities.name: DEVELOPMENTAL PATHWAY
entities.description: Biological processes governing growth, whose increased expression in AF13 correlates with oxidative stress tolerance.

entities.name: OXIDATIVE STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a fungal isolate to survive and function under oxidative stress conditions.

entities.name: PLANT INFECTION
entities.description: The process by which Aspergillus flavus invades and colonizes plant tissues.

entities.name: MICROBIAL COMPETITION
entities.description: The struggle between Aspergillus flavus and other microorganisms for resources.

entities.name: ENERGY PRODUCTION
entities.description: The cellular process of generating energy, which is elevated in isolates with lower aflatoxin production.

entities.name: CELLULAR COMPONENT PRODUCTION
entities.description: The synthesis of cellular structures, which is elevated in isolates with lower aflatoxin production.

entities.name: OXIDATIVE DAMAGE REMEDIATION
entities.description: The process of repairing cellular damage caused by oxidative stress.

entities.name: HOST PLANT INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The complex biological relationship between the pathogen Aspergillus flavus and its host plants.
